titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/342

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=339 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:33.783738+00 | translation moa=340 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:33.759042+00

p.cl: Sciant omnes tam presentes quam futuri quod

concessi et hac
mea carta confirmaui Abbati et conuentui de Melros donacionem illam quam dominus Thomas de
Normanuill’ frater meus fecit eisdem caritatiue super terram de Mackistun’. Quam quid terram
dictus dominus Thomas de Normanuill’ frater meus de me tenet in escambium terre sue de Ane
gus . Vnde uolo et concedo quod dicti abbas et conuentus de Melros dictam terram de Mackistun’ cum
mesuagio . tofto . crofto . libertatibus et asiamentis . prout in carta quam dicti abbas et conuen
tus habent de dicto domino Thoma de Normanuill’ fratre meo continetur  integre . quiete et honori
fice imperpetuum teneant et possideant .

In cuius rei testimonium presenti scripto sigillum meum ap
poni feci .

Testibus . Dompno Radulfo abbate de Balmorinauch . domino Willelmo filio .P. comitis
de Dunbar . Domino Nichol’ Corbeth filio eiusdem .W. domino Willelmo de Normanuill’ rectore ecclesie de
Mackist’ fratre meo . domino Leone nepote meo et multis aliis .

Actum anno gracie . m . cc .
Quinquagesimo . mense Iulio.
Let all, both present and future, know that I, Guy de Normanville,

have granted, and by this my charter made firm, to the abbot and convent of Melrose the donation which the lord Thomas de Normanville, my brother, charitably made to the same in respect of the land of Maxton. The said lord Thomas de Normanville, my brother, holds this land of me in exchange for his land of Angus. Wherefore I will and allow that the said abbot and convent of Melrose may hold and possess of the said lord Thomas de Normanville, my brother, the said land of Maxton with messuage, toft, croft, liberties and easements, as it is set out in the charter that the said abbot and convent have from the said lord Thomas de Normanville my brother, fully, quitely and honourably for ever.
In testimony of this matter I have caused my seal to be applied to this document.

As witnesses, Dom Ralph the abbot of Balmerino, the lord William son of Patrick the earl of Dunbar, the lord Nicholas Corbet, son of the same William, the lord William de Normanville the rector of the church of Maxton, my brother, the lord Leo my nephew, and many others.
Enacted in the year of grace 1240 in the month of July.
